method: multi-agent verify + adversarial falsification pass (fresh contexts); verifier corroborated Simo's own X post via TechCrunch, PYMNTS, Benzinga, CNBC, Bloomberg, Axios; falsifier independently checked the succession-detail claim's independence (traced it to a single WSJ origin relayed by TechCrunch/Fortune), verified quote consistency, and confirmed the April medical-leave and July step-down are correctly sequenced as two distinct events
origin_items: data/raw/2026-07-10/techcrunch-ai.json — Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI's no. 2 role
data/raw/2026-07-10/the-verge-ai.json — Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI's AGI work due to illness
data/raw/2026-07-10/wired-ai.json — OpenAI's CEO of AGI Deployment, Fidji Simo, Is Stepping Down
data/raw/2026-07-10/cnbc-technology.json — OpenAI exec Fidji Simo says she's stepping down due to chronic illness, will transition to advisor

claims.text: Fidji Simo announced on 9 July 2026 that she is stepping down from her full-time OpenAI role and transitioning to a part-time advisor
claims.reasoning: Anchored by her own primary statement (X post), independently corroborated by differently-fetched outlets (TechCrunch, PYMNTS, Benzinga, CNBC) -- comfortably clears the >=2-independent-source bar even before counting the primary-statement route.

claims.text: Her role, CEO of Applications (later CEO of AGI Deployment), was OpenAI's second-highest-ranking executive position
claims.reasoning: Original title confirmed via OpenAI's own May 2025 announcement blog post (Tier-0); title evolution across coverage explained, not contradictory.

claims.text: Simo attributed the decision to a severe flare-up of chronic illness (POTS, managed since 2019), medical leave began April 2026
claims.reasoning: Self-disclosed by Simo in her own words, quote consistency independently checked by the falsifier across multiple differently-fetched outlets (Fortune, CNBC, PYMNTS, Benzinga); April leave and July step-down confirmed as two distinct, correctly-sequenced events, not duplicate reporting.

claims.text: Sam Altman responded publicly expressing sadness and gratitude
claims.reasoning: Quote captured consistently across independent outlets.

claims.text: Responsibilities to be split among Greg Brockman, Sarah Friar, and Jason Kwon (per WSJ, relayed by TechCrunch)
claims.reasoning: Falsifier traced this detail to a single origin (Wall Street Journal reporting), relayed without independent confirmation by TechCrunch and Fortune -- per the independence rule, N outlets citing the same original report count as one source. No OpenAI statement or second independently-reporting outlet found. Kept single-source and explicitly attributed to WSJ/TechCrunch in the published text, not stated as OpenAI-confirmed fact.

notes: No OpenAI blog post or newsroom statement specific to this departure was found (only the original May 2025 appointment post exists as background) -- this remains a primary-statement (her own post) plus independent-press story, not a Tier-0-company-statement story. No conflicting account of the departure reason was found in either pass; reporting is consistent that this is genuinely health-related per her own disclosure, not performance-related -- did not reach 'disputed'. CNBC, Axios, and Bloomberg direct fetches were blocked (403) for both agents; those claims rest on cross-corroboration via TechCrunch, PYMNTS, and Benzinga fetches plus search-index confirmation.
